subject: Optimization Policy CSIT tests
commit_message:
Optimization Policy CSIT tests

This CSIT tests requires an entry in the database.  I modified the
sql script in the PE packages.  That commit should be merged in order
for this to pass

I had the wrong variable name for ${policy_name} in the template.  I
fixed this but I am not able to test locally so I cant be 100% sure this
will resolve the issue

I added fixes for the 2 failures.  I think the createPolicy failed due
to an invalid model but I see the model being inserted in the pap log.
The only thing I can think of is that it does not like the camel case
for hpaPolicy and the all caps for CSIT version so I just made these all
lower case in the request template

Change-Id: I59fe578c57ab927fe356071a5b4a28bd987fafd7
Issue-ID: POLICY-639
Signed-off-by: Michael Mokry <mm117s@att.com>

Patch Set 5:

As soon as I click the submit, the merge is there. So it should have showed up. It seems though that HPA created, there's still a bug in the test, and other policies failed to be created. Any ideas?

Patch Set 5:

Looks like the failures are now due to the following error...

Received response from policy PE300 - Data Issue: Invalid Template.  The template name, ClosedLoopControlName was not found in the dictionary.

so it looks like the template for the ops policies is missing.. this is essentially the same kind of error as invalid model for Config policies.  The HPA create policy passed so thats good :) but now the getCOnfig call for it failed.. I should be able to fix that but I am not sure if anything changed for the OPS policies that would cause them to fail now.

Patch Set 5:

UPDATE:  I found the issue with the OPS policies not creating.  As I suspected the BRMS template is failing to upload.  The CSIT scripts use the policyEngineImport API to do this.  Frank made some changes to this API to support TOSCA model imports via API.. I am having him look into the failure.

Patch Set 5:

> UPDATE:  I found the issue with the OPS policies not creating.  As
 > I suspected the BRMS template is failing to upload.  The CSIT
 > scripts use the policyEngineImport API to do this.  Frank made some
 > changes to this API to support TOSCA model imports via API.. I am
 > having him look into the failure.

Franks changes are testing successfully for uploading the BRMS template in our lab but looking deeper into the logs I found that the drl file is not getting downloaded successfully using wget...

wget -O cl-amsterdam-template.drl https://git.onap.org/policy/drools-applications/plain/controlloop/templates/archetype-cl-amsterdam/src/main/resources/archetype-resources/src/main/resources/__closedLoopControlName__.drl
+ wget -O cl-amsterdam-template.drl https://git.onap.org/policy/drools-applications/plain/controlloop/templates/archetype-cl-amsterdam/src/main/resources/archetype-resources/src/main/resources/__closedLoopControlName__.drl
--2018-04-11 14:20:53--  https://git.onap.org/policy/drools-applications/plain/controlloop/templates/archetype-cl-amsterdam/src/main/resources/archetype-resources/src/main/resources/__closedLoopControlName__.drl
Resolving git.onap.org (git.onap.org)... failed: Name or service not known.
wget: unable to resolve host address 'git.onap.org'

Patch Set 5:

Gary, is this a glitch? This has worked for a long time. The URL works fine outside of this but did settings change such that git.onap.org is inaccessible?

Mike, the more times you type r3check the more it aborts and restarts. When you submitted the patch a job was in the queue waiting to run. There are a lot of jobs this time of day, just be a little more patient.
